content: A bee resting on a flower. I came across this little bee toward the end of the day. I’m not sure if it was asleep but it was not moving for a few minutes. Even after it started moving a bit I was able to take advantage and get several close up shots before it flew off.
summary: A bee resting on a flower. I came across this little bee toward the end of the day. I’m not sure if it was asleep but it was not moving for a few minutes. Even after it started moving a bit I was able to take advantage and get several close up shots before it flew off.
title: Resting Bee

content:
A few interesting links about AI and coding, a game I finished, and some miscellany…
Photography 🔗 2026 Outdoor Alabama Photo Contest
ADCNR hopes you enjoy the winning images of the 2026 Outdoor Alabama Photo Contest. The annual contest highlights some of the best photographers in the state as well as the beauty of Alabama’s natural landscapes and wildlife. Contest sponsors include Alabama State Parks, the Alabama Conservation Enforcement Officers Association and the Alabama Tourism Department.
AI and Coding Stories 🔗 Malus.sh
Finally, liberation from open source license obligations.
Our proprietary AI robots independently recreate any open source project from scratch. The result? Legally distinct code with corporate-friendly licensing. No attribution. No copyleft. No problems.
🔗 Can coding agents relicense open source through a “clean room” implementation of code?
I see this as a microcosm of the larger question around coding agents for fresh implementations of existing, mature code. This question is hitting the open source world first, but I expect it will soon start showing up in Compaq-like scenarios in the commercial world.
🔗 The L in “LLM” Stands for Lying
This leaves the engineers in question in an awkward spot however. In order for vibe-coding to be acceptable and justifiable, they have to consider their own output disposable, highly uncreative, and not worthy of credit.
Video Games 🔗 Big Hops
I finished playing through Big Hops on Switch 2. It’s a solid 7/10 3D platformer. I liken it to the Mario Odyssey equivalent of Super Lucky’s Tale. For someone like me who has an insatiable thirst for more 3D platforming, this one was worth checking out. You play as Hop the frog, and use his jumping and tongue prowess to traverse levels and solve puzzles. Not super difficult but there are some surprising challenge areas.
TIL 🔗 Cow Valve Replacements: 5 Important Facts
